page: Microsoft Azure E96-48ads_v5 is an EADSv5-series virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The memory configuration is highly dense, offering 672.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 14.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 3686 GB of temporary disk capacity, and the instance supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory subsystem performance is a notable highlight; the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10% tier, although large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50% range. Single-core execution speeds, measured by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, align with average tier performance.

While the server excels in multi-threaded decompression and image processing, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. Large language model inference text generation for a 70B model is poor, registering in the bottom 10% at 2.35 tokens per second, which reflects the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage, this instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large relational databases, enterprise applications, and data caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EADSv5 family, featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, representing a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 3686 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU and top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 10%. However, single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. Due to the absence of a GPU, the instance is poorly suited for large-scale LLM text generation, but its high memory density and local storage make it highly effective for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads and enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, substantial local storage, and strong multi-core processing for enterprise database workloads.

672.0 GB of RAM providing a high 14.0 GB per vCPU allocation
3686 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory performance with PassMark memory scores in the top 10%
